title: Neobartsia elachophylla
taxonomy: Neobartsia elachophylla is a species of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. This classification places it within the order Lamiales. Neobartsia elachophylla is a parasitic plant, drawing nutrients from its host plants. It is native to South America, specifically in the tropical regions. The exact classification of Neobartsia elachophylla is further detailed within the plant's phylogenetic family and genus.
uses: Neobartsia elachophylla is not well-documented in terms of its medicinal uses. intro: Neobartsia elachophylla is a plant species belonging to the family Orobanchaceae, commonly known as a parasitic flowering plant. The plant has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing symptoms of various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. Neobartsia elachophylla contains a variety of bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
